title: Past Tenses
shortSummary: Learn the two essential past tenses — the pretérito indefinido for completed actions and the pretérito imperfecto for descriptions and habitual past actions. Being able to talk about the past transforms your storytelling ability.
whyItMatters: Without past tenses, you are stuck in the present. You cannot tell stories, describe experiences, or explain what happened yesterday. The two main past tenses in Spanish work together — one for actions that happened, one for the backdrop and context.
coreConcepts: Common irregular preterites: fui, tuve, hice, dije, puse
When to use indefinido vs imperfecto
Time markers: ayer, la semana pasada, cuando era niño
The pretérito perfecto (he comido) for recent past
